message:
test(fcp-conformance/host-cancellation): pin track/cancel/complete state machine + br-jdaro defense (br-h3nsg)

CancellationController is the host's per-operation cancellation
tracker. Two NORMATIVE properties drive its design:

1. State machine (br-2653): track + cancel -> Cancelled; second
   cancel -> Pending (idempotent); cancel after complete -> TooLate;
   cancel of unknown operation -> ConnectorNotFound.
2. Principal-mismatch defense (br-jdaro): when track recorded an
   owner, cancel MUST reject any caller whose asserted_principal
   does not match — BEFORE any state mutation. Otherwise an
   attacker who guesses a client-chosen operation_id could cancel
   anyone's operations.

Zero conformance coverage despite both properties being explicitly
named in source.

Adds 12 tests against the public fcp_host::CancellationController
API:

- cancel_of_tracked_operation_returns_cancelled_outcome
- second_cancel_returns_pending_outcome (idempotency)
- cancel_after_complete_returns_too_late
- cancel_of_unknown_operation_returns_error
- br_jdaro_principal_mismatch_rejects_before_state_mutation
  (THE security property — the rejected attempt MUST NOT leave the
  tracked op marked cancel_requested; legitimate owner can still
  cancel afterwards)
- br_jdaro_principal_mismatch_with_none_asserted_is_rejected (a
  caller who fails to assert any principal cannot bypass the
  match check)
- legacy_none_owner_is_permissive_for_any_caller (back-compat path)
- audit_log_records_every_successful_cancel
- audit_log_records_too_late_cancel_attempts (forensic capture)
- remove_after_complete_drops_tracking_entry (no stale state)
- is_cancel_requested_returns_false_for_untracked_operation (no
  panic on unknown id)
- track_then_complete_then_is_cancel_requested_remains_false

All 12 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
host_cancellation_controller_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
